title: Mustardy Silky Canadian Pumpkin En-Croute
description: A rich, date-night Canadian dish featuring roasted pumpkin, silky mustard sauce, and crispy en-croute pastry, all infused with the subtle flavors of cumin and coriander. This Ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly recipe is perfect for a cozy evening in.
instructions: Mix the flour, mustard seeds, coriander powder, cumin powder, garlic powder, and salt in a small bowl.
Add the unsalted butter and mix until crumbly. Gradually add the water and mix until a dough forms. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Peel and dice the pumpkin into 1-inch cubes. Place on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until tender. Let cool completely.
Mix the roasted pumpkin, yogurt, and turbinado sugar in a blender or food processor until smooth. Season with salt to taste.

Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch. Spoon the pumpkin mixture onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges. Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges to seal. Use a knife or pastry brush to create a decorative edge.
Place the en-croute on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush the top with a little water and sprinkle with turbinado sugar.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving. Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve warm.
Slice the en-croute into 6-8 wedges and serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh parsley if desired.
